elements.sent-2411-002: We investigate several properties of Kronecker (direct, tensor) products of graphs that are planar and $3$-connected (polyhedral, $3$-polytopal).
elements.sent-2411-003: This class of graphs was recently characterised and constructed by the second author \cite{mafkpr .}
elements.sent-2411-004: Our main result is that cancellation holds for the Kronecker product of graphs when the product is planar and $3$-connected (it is known that Kronecker cancellation may fail in general).
elements.sent-2411-005: Equivalently, polyhedral graphs are Kronecker products in at most one way.
elements.sent-2411-006: This is a special case of the deep and interesting question, open in general, of Kronecker product cancellation for simple graphs: when does $A\w C\simeq B\w C$ imply $A\simeq B$? We complete our investigation on simultaneous products by characterising and constructing the planar graphs that are Cartesian products in two distinct ways, and the planar, $3$-connected graphs that are both Kronecker and Cartesian products.
elements.sent-2411-007: The other type of results we obtain are in extremal graph theory.
elements.sent-2411-008: We classify the polyhedral Kronecker products that are either face-regular or vertex-regular graphs.
elements.sent-2411-009: The face-regular ones are certain quadrangulations of the sphere, while the vertex-regular ones are certain cubic graphs (duals of maximal planar graphs).
elements.sent-2411-010: We also characterise, and iteratively construct, the face-regular subclass of graphs minimising the number of vertices of degree $3$.
